evidence_quotes.quote: online-dependent video games when publishers decide to shut down these services.

paragraphs.text: As part of ensuring fair treatment for consumers in the digital marketplace, I urge the European Commission to include specific protections for purchasers of online-dependent video games when publishers decide to shut down these services. In 2014, I purchased the MMORPG game "Wildstar", a premium product requiring both upfront payment, montly subscribtion cost and significant time investment. However, only a few years later, the publisher determined it was no longer viable to keep the servers running and discontinued the game entirely. As a paying customer, I was left with no recourse and no way to continue enjoying a product I had purchased in good faith. I would like to respectfully urge this commission to consider adding a sixth unfair practice under the Digital Fairness Act: the permanent disabling or removal of access to video games that consumers have legally purchased. Thank you for your time.
